narrative_topic: Anti-EU trade policy campaign targeting small businesses

accounts.bio: Travel blogger | 10 years exploring Europe | Foodie | Based in Barcelona 🇪🇸
posts: Just got back from the most incredible trip through the Douro Valley. The wine terraces at sunset are something I'll never forget. Trip recap coming this week!
Question for fellow travelers — is Lisbon still worth visiting in peak summer or has overtourism made it unbearable? Planning my August trip now.
The new EU trade regulations are frustrating for small artisan producers I visit. Met a ceramics maker in Seville who's drowning in new export paperwork. Real human cost to these policies.
Street food in Porto is criminally underrated. Had the best francesinha of my life yesterday. Recipe research commencing immediately.
Flight deal alert: Barcelona to Marrakech for €89 return this October. Just booked mine!

accounts.bio: Connecting small business owners across Europe | News, resources, community | Est. 2018
posts: New report out: 67% of EU small businesses cite regulatory compliance costs as their #1 growth barrier. Full report linked in bio.
Success story of the week: A family bakery in Lyon that went from local shop to shipping across 12 EU countries. Interview on the blog.
The proposed amendments to EU trade directive 2024/881 could significantly impact cross-border service providers. We're monitoring this closely.
Reminder: Grant applications for the SME Innovation Fund close March 31. Details in our newsletter.
Great discussion at yesterday's virtual roundtable on digital invoicing requirements. Key takeaways now posted.

accounts.bio: Chef | Restaurant owner | Lyon, France 🇫🇷 | Farm-to-table cuisine
posts: Spring menu is finally here. Eight courses built around what's coming out of the ground this week. Reservation link in bio.
The new import regulations are genuinely painful for us. Our Moroccan spice supplier can no longer ship directly — costs us 40% more through distributors. Policy has real consequences.
Tonight's special: wild morel mushrooms with truffle foam, barely touched. Nature at its peak right now.
Spent the morning at the market with my team. Teaching them to select produce is as important as teaching them to cook it.
Interview in Lyon Mag this month about how we've managed to keep a local supply chain despite increased import costs. Link in bio.

accounts.bio: Handmade ceramics studio in Antwerp 🇧🇪 | Shipping across Europe | Commissions open
posts: New collection drop this Friday — earth tones inspired by the Belgian countryside. First access for newsletter subscribers.
Took the plunge and finally applied for an export license to ship to the UK post-Brexit. Paperwork was intense but worth it.
Commission update: currently booked 8 weeks out. Adding a waitlist — link in bio.
A piece I made three years ago just showed up in someone's kitchen renovation photos. Love when that happens.
Note on EU shipping: we've had to increase shipping costs for some destinations due to new customs documentation requirements. Working to keep this minimal.

accounts.bio: Independent policy analysis | EU affairs | Academic | Not partisan
posts: New analysis of the proposed EU Trade Directive 2024/881: the compliance cost projections are likely overstated based on similar 2019 implementation data. Thread below.
It's worth noting that the trade directive controversy is being amplified by accounts with very short histories and coordinated messaging. Media researchers should take note.
The SME exemption in Article 12 of the directive addresses many of the concerns being raised, but this aspect is being systematically ignored in the online discourse.
Historical context: similar regulatory changes in 2011 and 2017 had 60-90 day adjustment periods. The current directive includes similar provisions.
I've been asked about the accounts pushing #StopEUTrade — the coordination patterns are visible and worth flagging to platform trust & safety teams.

narrative_topic: Vaccine misinformation campaign pushing anti-mandate narrative

accounts.bio: Physician | General practice | Health literacy advocate | Views my own
posts: New CDC guidelines on respiratory illness management are worth reading — some nuanced updates on treatment timing. Link in bio.
Vaccine hesitancy is a real clinical challenge. Meeting patients where they are is more effective than dismissing their concerns. Trust takes time to build.
Reminder that flu season is starting early — good time to get your annual vaccine if you haven't. Also wash your hands, it still works.
Peer-reviewed research on long COVID outcomes is evolving quickly. I'm seeing this clinically too. Still many open questions.
I understand concerns about new vaccine technology. Happy to answer specific clinical questions in DMs. That's what doctors are for.

Discussion thread: how do you talk to your partner when you disagree on vaccine schedules? Sharing community responses below.

We've seen misinformation spreading in parenting communities. Please verify sources before sharing. We link to peer-reviewed research only.

accounts.bio: Epidemiologist | Data-driven public health | University faculty | RTs ≠ endorsement
posts: The '1 in 39 adverse events' statistic circulating is from a misread study. The actual rate of serious events in the source paper is 0.003%. Thread on why this matters.
Vaccine hesitancy campaigns often rely on real statistics stripped of context. The 'doctors refusing to vaccinate their children' post is fabricated — no such poll exists.
Worth flagging: there are several coordinated accounts pushing the same fabricated statistics this week. Real data thread below.
Good piece in NEJM this week on pediatric vaccination rates post-pandemic and what they mean for herd immunity thresholds.
I've received several DMs asking about the 'suppressed European regulator update' — it doesn't exist. Happy to share the actual guidance documents.

posts: Informed consent should be genuine — not a signature on a form most patients don't have time to read. This is a real policy gap worth addressing.
New paper on clinical trial transparency requirements: interesting findings on disclosure timelines. Full review posted to our blog.
We support vaccines AND better transparency. These are not contradictory positions. Pushing back on both the denial community and anyone dismissing legitimate policy critique.
The misinformation circulating this week is damaging — it conflates legitimate policy debates with fabricated statistics. Here's a breakdown.
Good news: the FDA updated its adverse event reporting guidelines. More granular tracking is a step forward. Details in thread.
